_provenance.source: docs/raw/V20_V30_Users_Manual_Oct86.pdf, Section 12 (PDF pages 46-225; printed page 12-N = PDF page N+45)
_provenance.method: Visual transcription of scanned pages by 11 parallel extraction agents (2026-07-11); encoding byte-boxes read from page images, NOT from OCR text. Structural validation + opcode spot-checks passed. Manual misprints transcribed as printed and flagged in _uncertain.
_provenance.coverage_note: 288 instruction-form records; Appendix A index lists 304 page-refs, the difference being dual-mnemonic index aliases (e.g. BC/BL, BE/BZ listed once per name) and the duplicated OUT DW,acc printing.

_conventions.clocks: Clock counts assume instruction bytes are already prefetched and include decoding, EA generation, operand fetch, and execution (Section 12 intro, PDF p46). uPD70116 = V30 (16-bit bus, odd/even address variants); uPD70108 = V20.
_conventions.mem_field_mod01_10: same base plus disp8 (mod=01) or disp16 (mod=10); mem=110 becomes BP+disp
_conventions.encoding_notation: One string per instruction byte. Fixed bits are 0/1 characters; single-bit fields inline (W, s); multi-bit fields space-separated names (mod, reg, mem, sreg); parenthesized bytes are optional (present per mod/W).

While CW != 0, the following block comparison instruction (CMPBK or CMPM) is executed and CW is decremented (-1). If the comparison result is CY != 1 the instruction terminates. CW is checked immediately before each execution of the block comparison instruction; if CW = 0 the first time, the block instruction is not executed at all. CY contents immediately before the first execution are don't care.

While CW != 0, the following block comparison instruction (CMPBK or CMPM) is executed and CW is decremented (-1). If the comparison result is CY = 1 the instruction terminates. CW is checked immediately before each execution; if CW = 0 the first time, the block instruction is not executed at all. CY contents immediately before the first execution are don't care.

While CW != 0, the following instruction is executed and CW is decremented (-1). REP is used with MOVBK, LDM, STM, OUTM, or INM; the Z flag is disregarded. REPZ/REPE is used with CMPBK or CMPM; the loop exits if the comparison result is Z != 1 or when CW becomes 0. CW is checked immediately before execution; if CW = 0 the first time, the block instruction is not executed at all. Z contents immediately before the first execution are don't care.

Bit length from 8-bit register of second operand (0=1 bit, 15=16 bits; only lower 4 bits valid). Destination: byte offset via DS1:IY plus bit offset in 8-bit register of first operand (lower 4 bits valid, max 15). After transfer, IY and first-operand register auto-updated to point to next bit field. Bit field data may overlap byte boundary. For correct operation the upper four bits of the 8-bit registers used as first and second operands must be set to 0.

Bit length from 4-bit immediate (0=1 bit, 15=16 bits; valid only 0-15). Destination: byte offset via DS1:IY plus bit offset in 8-bit register of first operand (lower 4 bits valid, max 15). IY and first-operand register auto-updated to next bit field. Bit field may overlap byte boundary. For correct operation, set the upper four bits of the 8-bit register used as the first operand to 0.

Source: segment base DS0, byte offset via IX, bit offset from 8-bit register of first operand (lower 4 bits valid, max 15). Bit length from 8-bit register of second operand (0=1 bit, 15=16 bits; lower 4 bits valid). Remaining upper bits of AW zeroed. After transfer, IX and first-operand register updated to next bit field. Bit field may overlap byte boundary. Upper 4 bits of the 8-bit registers used as first and second operands must be set to 0.

Source: DS0 segment, byte offset via IX, bit offset from 8-bit register of first operand (lower 4 bits valid, max 15). Bit length from 4-bit immediate of second operand (0=1 bit, 15=16 bits; valid only 0-15). After transfer, IX and first-operand register updated to next bit field. Bit field may overlap byte boundary. Set the upper 4 bits of the 8-bit register used as the first operand to 0.

instructions.notes: Also written ADD4S (no operand). Adds packed BCD string at IX to packed BCD string at IY; length in CL (number of BCD digits), 1 to 254 digits. When digit count is odd (CL=odd), Z and CY may not be set correctly: Z not set unless upper 4 bits of highest byte are all zero, CY not set unless carry out of upper 4 bits of highest byte, and upper 4 bits of highest byte of result are undefined. Destination string must be in segment specified by DS1; segment override prohibited for destination. Source default segment DS0; segment override possible for source.

instructions.operation: temp <- AW; When temp / (mem8) <= FFH: AH <- temp % (mem8), AL <- temp / (mem8); When temp / (mem8) > FFH: (SP-1,SP-2) <- PSW, (SP-3,SP-4) <- PS, (SP-5,SP-6) <- PC, SP <- SP-6, IE <- 0, BRK <- 0, PS <- (003H,002H), PC <- (001H,000H)
instructions.notes: When quotient exceeds FFH the vector 0 interrupt is generated; quotient and remainder become undefined. Especially occurs when divisor is 0. Fractional quotient rounded off.

instructions.operation: temp <- AW; When temp / reg8 <= FFH: AH <- temp % reg8, AL <- temp / reg8; When temp / reg8 > FFH: (SP-1,SP-2) <- PSW, (SP-3,SP-4) <- PS, (SP-5,SP-6) <- PC, SP <- SP-6, IE <- 0, BRK <- 0, PS <- (003H,002H), PC <- (001H,000H)
instructions.notes: When quotient exceeds FFH (capacity of AL) the vector 0 interrupt is generated and quotient and remainder become undefined; usually occurs when divisor is 0. Fractional quotient is rounded off.

instructions.operation: temp <- DW,AW; When temp / (mem16) > FFFFH: (SP-1,SP-2) <- PSW, (SP-3,SP-4) <- PS, (SP-5,SP-6) <- PC, SP <- SP-6, IE <- 0, BRK <- 0, PS <- (003H,002H), PC <- (001H,000H); All other times: DW <- temp % (mem16), AL <- temp / (mem16)
instructions.notes: Prose states quotient is stored in AW and remainder in DW. When quotient exceeds FFFFH the vector 0 interrupt is generated; quotient and remainder become undefined. Especially occurs when divisor is 0.

instructions.operation: temp <- DW,AW; When temp / reg16 > FFFFH: (SP-1,SP-2) <- PSW, (SP-3,SP-4) <- PS, (SP-5,SP-6) <- PC, SP <- SP-6, IE <- 0, BRK <- 0, PS <- (003H,002H), PC <- (001H,000H); All other times: DW <- temp % reg16, AW <- temp / reg16
instructions.notes: Quotient in AW, remainder in DW. When quotient exceeds FFFFH the vector 0 interrupt is generated and quotient and remainder become undefined; most often occurs when divisor is 0. Fractional quotient rounded off.

instructions.operation: temp <- AW; When temp / (mem8) > 0 and (mem8) > 7FH or temp / (mem8) < 0 and temp / (mem8) > 0-7FH-1: (SP-1,SP-2) <- PSW, (SP-3,SP-4) <- PS, (SP-5,SP-6) <- PC, SP <- SP-6, IE <- 0, BRK <- 0, PS <- (003H,002H), PC <- (001H,000H); All other times: AH <- temp % (mem8), AL <- temp / (mem8)
instructions.notes: Maximum positive quotient +127 (7FH), minimum negative quotient -127 (81H). When quotient exceeds either maximum, quotient and remainder become undefined and vector 0 interrupt is generated (especially when divisor is 0). Fractional quotient rounded off. Remainder has same sign as dividend.

instructions.operation: temp <- CL, while temp != 0, repeat operation, CY <- MSB of reg, reg <- reg x 2 + CY, temp <- temp - 1
temp <- CL, while temp != 0, repeat operation, CY <- MSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) x 2 + CY, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: n = number of shifts.

instructions.operation: temp <- imm8, while temp != 0, repeat operation, CY <- MSB of reg, reg <- reg x 2 + CY, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: The register's MSB is shifted to the CY flag and to the LSB.

instructions.operation: temp <- imm8, while temp != 0, repeat operation, CY <- MSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) x 2 + CY, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: n = number of shifts. The memory location's MSB is shifted to the CY flag and to the LSB.

instructions.operation: CY <- LSB of reg, reg <- reg / 2, MSB of reg <- CY; MSB of reg != bit following MSB of reg: V <- 1; MSB of reg = bit following MSB of reg: V <- 0
instructions.notes: If the MSB of the register changes, V is set; if it stays the same, V is cleared.

instructions.operation: CY <- LSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) / 2, MSB of (mem) <- CY; MSB of (mem) != bit following MSB of (mem): V <- 1; MSB of (mem) = bit following MSB of (mem): V <- 0
instructions.notes: If the MSB of the addressed memory changes, V is set; if it stays the same, V is cleared.

instructions.operation: temp <- CL, while CL != 0, repeat operation, CY <- LSB of reg, reg <- reg / 2, MSB of reg <- CY, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: Operation line as printed says 'while CL != 0' (other variable-bit forms print 'while temp != 0').

instructions.operation: temp <- CL, while temp != 0, repeat operation, CY <- LSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) / 2, MSB of (mem) <- CY, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: n = number of shifts.

instructions.operation: temp <- imm8, while temp != 0, repeat operation, CY <- LSB of reg, reg <- reg / 2, MSB of reg <- CY, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: The register's LSB is shifted to the MSB and the CY flag.

instructions.operation: temp <- imm8, while temp != 0, repeat operation, CY <- LSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) / 2, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: n = number of shifts. The memory location's LSB is shifted to the MSB as well as to the CY flag. Printed operation formula omits the 'MSB of (mem) <- CY' line present in other ROR forms.

instructions.description: Rotate left with carry, register, single bit
instructions.operation: tmpcy <- CY, CY <- MSB of reg, reg <- reg x 2 + tmpcy; MSB of reg = CY: V <- 0; MSB of reg != CY: V <- 1
instructions.notes: Rotation includes the CY flag. If the register's MSB changes, V is set; if it stays the same, V is cleared.

instructions.description: Rotate left with carry, memory, single bit
instructions.operation: tmpcy <- CY, CY <- MSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) x 2 + tmpcy; MSB of (mem) = CY: V <- 0; MSB of (mem) != CY: V <- 1
instructions.notes: Rotation includes the CY flag. If the MSB of the memory location changes, V is set; if it stays the same, V is cleared.

instructions.description: Rotate left with carry, register, variable bit
instructions.operation: temp <- CL, while temp != 0, repeat operation, tmpcy <- CY, CY <- MSB of reg, reg <- reg x 2 + tmpcy, temp <- temp - 1

clocks.value: 7 + n, where n = number of shifts

instructions.notes: Rotates the 8- or 16-bit register left by the number in CL. Rotation includes the CY flag.

instructions.description: Rotate left with carry, memory, variable bit
instructions.operation: temp <- CL, while temp != 0, repeat operation, tmpcy <- CY, CY <- MSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) x 2 + tmpcy, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: n = number of shifts. Rotates the 8- or 16-bit memory location left by the number in CL; rotation includes the CY flag.

instructions.description: Rotate left with carry, register, multibit
instructions.operation: temp <- imm8, while temp != 0, repeat operation, tmpcy <- CY, CY <- MSB of reg, reg <- reg x 2 + tmpcy, temp <- temp - 1

clocks.value: 7 + n, where n = number of shifts

instructions.notes: Rotates the register left by the number of bits given by the 8-bit immediate. Rotation includes the CY flag.

instructions.description: Rotate left with carry, memory, multibit
instructions.operation: temp <- imm8, while temp != 0, repeat operation, tmpcy <- CY, CY <- MSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) x 2 + tmpcy, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: n = number of shifts. Rotates the memory location left by the number of bits given by the 8-bit immediate; rotation includes the CY flag.

instructions.description: Rotate right with carry, register, single bit
instructions.operation: tmpcy <- CY, CY <- LSB of reg, reg <- reg / 2, MSB of reg <- tmpcy, MSB of reg != bit following MSB of reg: V <- 1, MSB of reg = bit following MSB of reg: V <- 0
instructions.notes: Rotates right (including the CY flag) by one bit. If the MSB changes, V is set; if unchanged, V is cleared.

instructions.description: Rotate right with carry, memory, single bit
instructions.operation: tmpcy <- CY, CY <- LSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) / 2, MSB of (mem) <- tmpcy, MSB of (mem) != bit following MSB of (mem): V <- 1, MSB of (mem) = bit following MSB of (mem): V <- 0
instructions.notes: Rotates the memory location right (including the CY flag) by one bit. If the MSB changes, V is set; if unchanged, V is cleared.

instructions.description: Rotate right with carry, register, variable bit
instructions.operation: temp <- CL, while temp != 0, repeat operation, tmpcy <- CY, CY <- LSB of reg, reg <- reg / 2, MSB of reg <- tmpcy, temp <- temp - 1

clocks.value: 7 + n, where n = number of shifts

instructions.notes: Rotates the register right (including the CY flag) by the number in CL.

instructions.description: Rotate right with carry, memory, variable bit
instructions.operation: temp <- CL, while temp != 0, repeat operation, tmpcy <- CY, CY <- LSB of (mem), reg <- reg / 2, MSB of (mem) <- tmpcy, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: n = number of shifts. Rotates the memory location right (including the CY flag) by the number in CL. Manual's operation formula prints 'reg <- reg / 2' where '(mem) <- (mem) / 2' is clearly intended.

instructions.description: Rotate right with carry, register, multibit
instructions.operation: temp <- imm8, while temp != 0, repeat operation, tmpcy <- CY, CY <- LSB of reg, reg <- reg / 2, MSB of reg <- tmpcy, temp <- temp - 1

clocks.value: 7 + n, where n = number of shifts

instructions.notes: Rotates the register right (including the CY flag) by the number of bits given by the 8-bit immediate.

instructions.description: Rotate right with carry, memory multibit
instructions.operation: temp <- imm8, while temp != 0, repeat operation, tmpcy <- CY, CY <- LSB of (mem), (mem) <- (mem) / 2, MSB of (mem) <- tmpcy, temp <- temp - 1
instructions.notes: n = number of shifts. Rotates the memory location right (including the CY flag) by the number of bits given by the 8-bit immediate. Flag table prints V=U here although RORC reg,imm8 prints V=X.

instructions.operation: (SP - 1, SP - 2) <- PC, SP <- SP - 2, PC <- PC + disp
instructions.notes: Saves the PC to the stack and adds the 16-bit displacement to the PC. Enables calls to any address within the current segment.

instructions.operation: (SP - 1, SP - 2) <- PC, SP <- SP - 2, PC <- regptr16
instructions.notes: Saves the PC to the stack and loads the value of the 16-bit register specified by the operand to the PC.

instructions.operation: (SP - 1, SP - 2) <- PS, (SP - 3, SP - 4) <- PC, SP <- SP - 4, PS <- seg, PC <- offset
instructions.notes: Saves the PS and PC to the stack. Loads the fourth and fifth instruction bytes to PS and the second and third bytes to PC. Enables calls to any address in any segment.
Saves the PC to the stack and loads the contents of the 16-bit memory location addressed by the operand to the PC.

instructions.operation: (SP - 1, SP - 2) <- PS, (SP - 3, SP - 4) <- PC, SP <- SP - 4, PS <- (memptr32 + 3, memptr32 + 2), PC <- (memptr32 + 1, memptr32)
instructions.notes: Saves the PS and PC to the stack. Loads the higher two bytes of the 32-bit memory operand to PS and the lower two bytes to PC.
Used for returning from intrasegment calls. Restores the PC from the stack. The assembler automatically distinguishes this instruction from the other RET instruction with no operand.

instructions.operation: PC <- (SP + 1, SP), PS <- (SP + 3, SP + 2), SP <- SP + 4
instructions.notes: Restores the PC and PS from the stack. Used for returning from intersegment calls. The assembler automatically distinguishes this instruction from the RET instruction without an operand.

instructions.description: Return from procedure, SP jump, same segment
instructions.operation: PC <- (SP + 1, SP), SP <- SP + 2, SP <- SP + pop-value
instructions.notes: Restores the PC from the stack and adds the 16-bit pop-value to SP; effective for discarding stacked parameters. Used for returning from intrasegment calls. The assembler automatically distinguishes this instruction from the other RET pop-value instruction.
Saves the contents of the 16-bit memory location addressed by the operand to the stack.

instructions.description: Return from procedure, SP jump, intersegment
instructions.operation: PC <- (SP + 1, SP), PS <- (SP + 3, SP + 2), SP <- SP + 4, SP <- SP + pop-value
instructions.notes: Restores the PC and PS from the stack and adds the 16-bit pop-value to SP; effective for discarding stacked parameters. Used for returning from intersegment calls. The assembler automatically distinguishes this instruction from the other RET pop-value instruction.
Saves the 16-bit register specified by the operand to the stack.
Saves the segment register specified by the operand to the stack.
Saves the PSW to the stack.
Saves eight 16-bit registers (AW, BW, CW, DW, SP, BP, IX, and IY) to the stack. Pushed SP value is the pre-push value (temp).
Saves the 16-bit immediate data to the stack addressed by SP.

instructions.operation: (SP - 1, SP - 2) <- Sign expansion of imm8, SP <- SP - 2
instructions.notes: Expands the sign of the 8-bit immediate data and saves it as 16-bit data to the stack addressed by SP.
Transfers the contents of the stack to the 16-bit memory location addressed by the operand.
Transfers the contents of the stack to the 16-bit register specified by the operand.

The Mode flag (MD) can only be modified by POP PSW during Native mode calls from 8080 Emulation mode (between BRKEM and RETEM). In Native mode outside of Emulation mode, MD remains set to 1 regardless of the stack contents. Do not alter MD during Native mode calls from Emulation mode, or during Native mode interrupt service routines that may interrupt Emulation mode execution.
Segment register except PS. External interrupts NMI and INT, and single-step breaks will not be acknowledged between this instruction and the next.
Restores the contents of the stack to AW, BW, CW, DW, BP, SP, IX, and IY. The stored SP value (at SP+7, SP+6) is skipped, not loaded.

instructions.operation: (SP-1, SP-2) <- BP, SP <- SP - 2, temp <- SP; When imm8 > 0, repeat 'imm8 - 1' times: { (SP-1, SP-2) <- (BP-1, BP-2); SP <- SP - 2 (*1); BP <- BP - 2 } and perform: { (SP-1, SP-2) <- temp; SP <- SP - 2 (*2) }; Then: BP <- temp, SP <- SP - imm16. Notes: When imm8=1, *1 is not performed; when imm8=0, *1 and *2 are not performed.
instructions.notes: Generates stack frames for block-structured high-level languages (Pascal, Ada). imm16 = size in bytes of local variable area; imm8 = lexical nesting level. Frame base address is set in BP. Old BP is saved to the stack; frame pointers of enclosing procedures are copied when lexical level > 1.

instructions.operation: SP <- BP, BP <- (SP + 1, SP), SP <- SP + 2
instructions.notes: Releases the last stack frame generated by PREPARE. A value pointing to the preceding frame is loaded in BP; the bottom of the frame value is loaded in SP.
Loads current PC plus 16-bit displacement to PC. Assembler generates this automatically when the branch address is in the current segment.
8-bit displacement sign-extended to 16 bits. Assembler generates this automatically when target is in current segment within +/-127 bytes.
Loads contents of the 16-bit memory location addressed by the operand to PC. Can branch to any address in the current segment.
Loads contents of the 16-bit register specified by the operand to PC. Can branch to any address in the current segment.
Loads 16-bit offset (bytes 2-3) to PC and 16-bit segment (bytes 4-5) to PS. Can branch to any address in any segment.
Loads upper two bytes of the 32-bit memory operand to PS and lower two bytes to PC. Can branch to any address in any segment.
Taken if V=0. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if V=1. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if CY=1. Shares entry with alias BL short-label. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if CY=1. Alias of BC short-label (same encoding).
Taken if CY=0. Shares entry with alias BNL short-label. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if CY=0. Alias of BNC short-label (same encoding).
Taken if Z=1. Shares entry with alias BZ short-label. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if Z=0. Shares entry with alias BNZ short-label. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if Z=0. Alias of BNE short-label (same encoding).
Taken if Z=1. Alias of BE short-label (same encoding).

instructions.operation: When CY OR Z = 0, PC <- PC + ext-disp8
instructions.notes: Taken if (CY OR Z)=0. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.

instructions.operation: When CY OR Z = 1, PC <- PC + ext-disp8
instructions.notes: Taken if (CY OR Z)=1. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if S=1. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if S=0. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if P=1. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.
Taken if P=0. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.

instructions.description: Branch if greater than or equal
instructions.operation: When S XOR V = 0, PC <- PC + ext-disp8
instructions.notes: Taken if (S XOR V)=0. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.

instructions.operation: When S XOR V = 1, PC <- PC + ext-disp8
instructions.notes: Taken if (S XOR V)=1. Branch range +/-127 bytes, current segment.

instructions.operation: When (S XOR V) OR Z = 0, PC <- PC + ext-disp8
instructions.notes: Adds the 8-bit (actually, sign-extended 16-bit) displacement to PC. Can branch to any address within +/-127 bytes of the instruction in the current segment. If condition unsatisfied, proceeds to next instruction.

instructions.description: Branch if less than or equal
instructions.operation: When (S XOR V) OR Z = 1, PC <- PC + ext-disp8
instructions.notes: Adds the 8-bit (actually, sign-extended 16-bit) displacement to PC. Can branch to any address within +/-127 bytes of the instruction in the current segment. If condition unsatisfied, proceeds to next instruction.

instructions.description: Decrement and branch if not zero and equal
instructions.operation: CW <- CW - 1; When CW != 0 and Z = 1, PC <- PC + ext-disp8
instructions.notes: Decrements 16-bit register CW; if CW is not zero and Z flag is set, adds the 8-bit (actually, sign-extended 16-bit) displacement to PC. Can branch within +/-127 bytes of the instruction in the current segment.

instructions.description: Decrement and branch if not zero and not equal
instructions.operation: CW <- CW - 1; When CW != 0 and Z = 0, PC <- PC + ext-disp8
instructions.notes: Decrements 16-bit register CW; if resultant CW is not 0 and Z flag is cleared, adds the 8-bit (actually, sign-extended 16-bit) displacement to PC. Can branch within +/-127 bytes of the instruction in the current segment.
If 16-bit register CW is 0, adds the 8-bit (actually sign-extended 16-bit) displacement to PC. Can branch within +/-127 bytes of the instruction in the current segment. Does not decrement CW.

instructions.description: Decrement and branch if not zero
instructions.operation: CW <- CW - 1; When CW != 0, PC <- PC + ext-disp8
instructions.notes: Decrements 16-bit register CW; if CW value is not zero, adds the 8-bit (actually, sign-extended 16-bit) displacement to PC. Can branch within +/-127 bytes of the instruction in the current segment.
Saves PSW, PS, and PC to the stack and resets IE and BRK flags to 0. Loads the lower two bytes and higher two bytes of vector 3 of the interrupt vector table to PC and PS respectively.

instructions.operation: (SP - 1, SP - 2) <- PSW; (SP - 3, SP - 4) <- PS; (SP - 5, SP - 6) <- PC; SP <- SP - 6; IE <- 0; BRK <- 0; PC <- (imm8 x4 + 1, imm8 x4); PS <- (imm8 x4 + 3, imm8 x4 + 2)
instructions.notes: Saves PSW, PS, and PC to the stack and resets IE and BRK flags to 0. Loads the lower two bytes and upper two bytes of the interrupt vector table entry (4 bytes) specified by the 8-bit immediate data to PC and PS respectively. Bytes value transcribed as printed ('1'); actual instruction length is 2 bytes (opcode + imm8), likely a manual misprint.

When V flag is set, saves PSW, PS, and PC to the stack, resets IE and BRK flags to 0, then loads lower/upper two bytes of vector 4 of the interrupt vector table to PC and PS. When V flag is reset, proceeds to the next instruction. Clock condition labels as printed appear misaligned (see uncertain).

instructions.operation: PC <- (SP + 1, SP); PS <- (SP + 3, SP + 2); PSW <- (SP + 5, SP + 4); SP <- SP + 6
instructions.notes: Restores stack contents to PC, PS, and PSW; used to return from interrupt processing. MD flag can only be modified by RETI during Native mode calls from 8080 Emulation mode (i.e. between execution of BRKEM and RETEM). In Native mode outside Emulation mode, MD remains set to 1 regardless of stack contents. Do not alter MD during Native mode calls from Emulation mode or during interrupt service routines that may interrupt Emulation mode execution. RETI should be used to exit Native mode service routines and return to Emulation mode, and should be the last instruction executed in the Native mode service routine.

instructions.operation: (SP - 1, SP - 2) <- PSW; (SP - 3, SP - 4) <- PS; (SP - 5, SP - 6) <- PC; SP <- SP - 6; MD <- 0, write enable MD; PS <- (imm8 x4 + 3, imm8 x4 + 2); PC <- (imm8 x4 + 1, imm8 x4)
instructions.notes: Starts emulation mode. Saves PSW, PS, and PC, resets MD bit to 0, and jumps to the emulation location addressed by the interrupt vector specified by the 8-bit immediate operand. After fetching the instruction code of the jumped interrupt service routine, the CPU interprets and executes the code as uPD8080AF instructions. Use RETEM or CALLN to return from emulation mode to native mode (uPD70108/70116): CALLN temporarily returns the program from Emulation to Native mode and RETEM completes Emulation mode.

clocks.when: interrupt condition fulfilled, uPD70116 odd addresses
interrupt condition fulfilled, uPD70116 even addresses
interrupt condition not fulfilled, uPD70116 odd addresses
interrupt condition not fulfilled, uPD70116 even addresses

instructions.transfers: When interrupt condition is fulfilled: 7; when not fulfilled: 2
instructions.notes: Checks whether the index value in reg16 is within the defined array bounds; initiates a BRK 5 when the index does not satisfy the condition. The definition region must be set beforehand in two words of memory: first word is the lower limit, second word is the upper limit. Flag operation applies only when the interrupt condition is fulfilled; when not fulfilled, flag operation is None.
Sets the halt state. The halt state is released by the RESET, NMI, or INT input.

instructions.operation: Wait until POLL pin is active low

clocks.value: 2 + 5n, where n = number of times POLL pin is sampled

instructions.notes: Keeps the CPU in the idle state until the POLL pin becomes an active low level.
Resets the IE flag and disables the external maskable interrupt input (INT). Does not disable the external non-maskable interrupt input (NMI) or software interrupt instructions.

Sets the IE flag and enables the external maskable interrupt input (INT). The system does not enter the interrupt-enable state until after executing the instruction immediately following EI. Manual prints the operation formula as 'EI <- 1' (evidently a misprint for IE <- 1).

In large-scale mode (S/LG = 0): outputs the buslock signal (/BUSLOCK) while the instruction immediately after BUSLOCK is being executed. When used with a block operation instruction with a repeat prefix, /BUSLOCK is kept active low until the end of the block operation. Hold request is inhibited while /BUSLOCK is active; effective when you do not want to acknowledge a hold request during block operations. In small-scale mode (S/LG = 1): the /BUSLOCK signal is not an output, but the instruction can be used to delay a hold acknowledge response until execution of the locked instruction is completed.

instructions.operation: No CPU operation; instruction executed by external floating point chip
instructions.notes: Used when the floating point arithmetic chip is connected externally. Causes the CPU to leave arithmetic processing to the floating point chip. When the floating point chip monitors this instruction, it treats the instruction as its own and executes it.

Used when the floating point arithmetic chip is externally connected. CPU leaves arithmetic processing to the floating point chip and instead carries out auxiliary processing: calculation of effective address, generation of physical addresses, and start of memory read cycles when necessary. The floating point chip, when it monitors this instruction, treats it as its own and executes it, selecting either the address information of the memory read cycle started by the CPU or both the address and read data depending on the type of instruction. The CPU does not use the read data on the data bus in the memory read cycle it initiated.

instructions.operation: No CPU operation; instruction executed by external floating point chip
instructions.notes: Used with an externally connected floating point arithmetic chip. Causes the CPU to leave processing to the floating point chip. When the floating point chip monitors this instruction, it interprets the instruction as its own and executes it.

Used with an externally connected floating point arithmetic chip. CPU leaves arithmetic processing to the floating point chip and instead carries out auxiliary processing: calculation of effective addresses, generation of physical addresses, and start of memory read cycles when necessary. The floating point chip treats the instruction as its own and executes it, selecting either the address information of the memory read cycle started by the CPU or both the address and read data depending on the type of instruction.
Causes the processor to do nothing for three clocks.

instructions.operation: (SP - 1, SP - 2) <- PSW; (SP - 3, SP - 4) <- PS; (SP - 5, SP - 6) <- PC; SP <- PS - 6 (as printed; evidently SP <- SP - 6); MD <- 1; PS <- (imm8 x4 + 3, imm8 x4 + 2); PC <- (imm8 x4 + 1, imm8 x4)
instructions.notes: When executed in emulation mode, the CPU interprets the instruction as a uPD8080AF command. The CPU saves PS, PC, and PSW to the stack (MD = 0 is also saved). Then the MD flag is set to 1. The interrupt vector specified by the 8-bit immediate data is loaded into PS and PC. Allows calling a native mode interrupt routine from emulation mode. The RETI command is used to return to emulation mode from the interrupt routine.

instructions.operation: Use sreg for memory operand access of following instruction
instructions.notes: When appended to the operand, specifies the segment register to be used for access of a memory operand expecting segment override. The segment override can also be defined by assembler directive ASSUME without writing the prefix directly. sreg: 00=DS1, 01=PS, 10=SS, 11=DS0.

instructions.operation: PC <- (SP + 1, SP); PS <- (SP + 3, SP + 2); PSW <- (SP + 5, SP + 4); SP <- SP + 6; write disable MD
instructions.notes: When executed in emulation mode, the CPU interprets this instruction as a uPD8080AF command. Restores the PS, PC, and PSW saved by the BRKEM command in the same manner as when returning from interrupt processing. When BRKEM is executed, the MD flag is write disabled, so the MD flag is not restored by executing the RETI or POP PSW instructions.
